mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate lower borrowing costs.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 70%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 150k signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings optimism and positive SPX returns within 5 trading days as investors price in higher future profits.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or t-test p-value >0.1 for mean return difference vs baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and consumer price inflation indicate weak aggregate demand, leading to negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as recession risks dominate market sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods with HOUST < 1350 and CPIAUCSL < 2.5%, or interaction effect size < 0.15 in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production above 110 signals strengthening manufacturing activity, generating positive SPX returns within 20 trading days as investors price in higher corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 55%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 110, or Sharpe ratio < 0.5 in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 12-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, causing negative SPX returns over 20 trading days as bond yields pressure equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5, or regression coefficient < -0.1 in 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening economic demand, producing negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as recession fears dominate market psychology.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods with GDPC1 < 0 and RSAFS < 525k, or interaction term p-value >0.2 in OLS model.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions, generating positive SPX returns within 5 trading days as investors anticipate wage-driven corporate profit growth.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 55%+ of instances where UNRATE < 3.5, or t-test p-value >0.1 for mean return difference vs baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production combined with falling oil prices (not in catalog) would signal weak demand, but since oil isn't available, we use INDPRO and CPIAUCSL to detect supply-side inflation pressures causing SPX sell-offs over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods with INDPRO > 109 and CPIAUCSL > 2.5, or interaction effect size < 0.1 in rolling 3-year regression.
